body:
![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The logic here is very similar to the `Task` and `Agent` cases. To avoid duplication and improve maintainability, consider extracting this logic into a helper function that can be shared by all three cases (`Task`, `Agent`, `spawn_agent`).

🟡 **`spawn_agent` and `wait` missing from PERMISSION_EXEMPT_TOOLS causes false permission prompts for Codex** `spawn_agent` (Codex's equivalent of Claude's `Task`/`Agent`) and `wait` (waits for a spawned sub-agent) are not included in `PERMISSION_EXEMPT_TOOLS`. When a Codex agent uses these tools, `startPermissionTimer` fires at `src/transcriptParser.ts:140-141`. After 7 seconds of JSONL silence (common while a sub-agent works), the timer callback at `src/timerManager.ts:84-91` finds `spawn_agent`/`wait` active and non-exempt, sending a false `agentToolPermission` bubble to the webview. The code already recognizes `spawn_agent` as a sub-agent tool (it sends `subagentClear` on completion at line 156–161, and `formatToolStatus` returns `"Subtask: ..."` at line 87–94), and `wait` produces `"Waiting on subtask"` — but both were omitted from the exempt set, unlike their Claude counterparts `Task` and `Agent`. ```suggestion export const PERMISSION_EXEMPT_TOOLS = new Set([ 'Task', 'Agent', 'AskUserQuestion', 'request_user_input', 'spawn_agent', 'wait', ]); ``` --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

🔴 **Single project scan timer prevents JSONL discovery when adapter changes after restore** `ensureProjectScan` at `src/fileWatcher.ts:116` early-returns if `projectScanTimerRef.current` is already set. In `PixelAgentsViewProvider.ts`, `restoreAgents` (line 120-135) runs first and may call `ensureProjectScan` with the restored agents' adapter (e.g., Codex scanning `~/.codex/sessions/`). The subsequent `ensureProjectScan` call at line 156 with the *currently configured* adapter becomes a no-op. If the user changes `pixel-agents.agentType` (e.g., from `codex` to `claude`), the scan continues monitoring only the old adapter's directory. For Codex agents (which rely entirely on scanning since `getExpectedJsonlFile()` returns `null` at `src/adapters/codexAdapter.ts:27-28`), this means newly created agents can never discover their JSONL files — they stay stuck with the placeholder `pending-.jsonl` path (`src/agentManager.ts:72`) and never receive tool or status updates. The same issue affects `launchNewTerminal` at `src/agentManager.ts:99-113`, which also calls `ensureProjectScan` but gets the no-op.
Prompt for agents ``` In src/fileWatcher.ts, the ensureProjectScan function uses a single projectScanTimerRef guard that prevents scanning multiple adapter directories. When restored agents use a different adapter than the currently configured one, only one directory gets scanned. The fix should either: 1. Support multiple concurrent scan timers (one per adapter/directory), keyed by the adapter name or project directory path, instead of a single projectScanTimerRef. This would require changing the timer ref from a single { current: ... } to a Map>. 2. Or, when ensureProjectScan is called with a different projectDir than the currently scanning one, cancel the old timer and start a new one for the new directory. 3. Or, combine both approaches: scan all active adapter directories. Files to change: - src/fileWatcher.ts: ensureProjectScan and scanForNewJsonlFiles need to support multiple directories/adapters - src/PixelAgentsViewProvider.ts: projectScanTimer type may need to change from single ref to Map - src/agentManager.ts: launchNewTerminal and restoreAgents pass the timer ref ```
--- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_

**들여쓰기 불일치 수정 필요.**

변경된 라인(8-18)과 변경되지 않은 라인(19-20) 사이에 들여쓰기 불일치가 있습니다. 여는 중괄호 `{`는 탭 3개로 들여쓰기되어 있지만, 닫는 중괄호 `}`는 탭 2개입니다. 또한 `preLaunchTask` 속성은 다른 속성(탭 4개)과 달리 탭 3개로 들여쓰기되어 있어 일관성이 없습니다.

JSON 자체는 유효하지만, 일관되지 않은 포맷은 향후 편집 시 혼란을 야기하거나 병합 충돌의 원인이 될 수 있습니다.

<details>
<summary>📐 들여쓰기 일관성 수정 제안</summary>

```diff
 	"configurations": [
-			{
-				"name": "Run Extension",
-				"type": "extensionHost",
-				"request": "launch",
-				"args": [
-					"--extensionDevelopmentPath=${workspaceFolder}",
-					"${workspaceFolder}"
-				],
-				"outFiles": [
-					"${workspaceFolder}/dist/**/*.js"
-				],
-			"preLaunchTask": "${defaultBuildTask}"
-		}
+		{
+			"name": "Run Extension",
+			"type": "extensionHost",
+			"request": "launch",
+			"args": [
+				"--extensionDevelopmentPath=${workspaceFolder}",
+				"${workspaceFolder}"
+			],
+			"outFiles": [
+				"${workspaceFolder}/dist/**/*.js"
+			],
+			"preLaunchTask": "${defaultBuildTask}"
+		}
 	]
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
		{
			"name": "Run Extension",
			"type": "extensionHost",
			"request": "launch",
			"args": [
				"--extensionDevelopmentPath=${workspaceFolder}",
				"${workspaceFolder}"
			],
			"outFiles": [
				"${workspaceFolder}/dist/**/*.js"
			],
			"preLaunchTask": "${defaultBuildTask}"
		}
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @.vscode/launch.json around lines 8 - 20, Fix the inconsistent indentation
inside the "Run Extension" launch configuration: align the opening brace for the
object and its closing brace to the same indentation level as the surrounding
launch configurations and make the "preLaunchTask" property's indentation match
the other properties ("name", "type", "request", "args", "outFiles"). Locate the
object with "name": "Run Extension" and adjust whitespace so all properties use
the same indentation (and the surrounding braces use that same level) to ensure
consistent formatting across the JSON file.
```

</details>

**경로 대소문자 정규화 추가 필요**

`path.normalize()`는 경로 구분자만 정규화하고 대소문자는 변경하지 않습니다. Windows와 대소문자 구분 없는 macOS에서 외부 에이전트(Claude Code, Codex)가 저장한 세션 메타데이터의 `cwd`와 VS Code의 `folder.uri.fsPath`가 다른 대소문자로 표현되면 `===` 비교에서 실패할 수 있습니다. 대소문자를 정규화하여 비교하세요.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@src/adapters/shared.ts` around lines 50 - 53, The equality check can fail due
to case differences; ensure paths are compared case-insensitively by normalizing
case (e.g., toLowerCase()) when normalizing paths. Update normalizeWorkspacePath
(or apply .toLowerCase() where normalizeWorkspacePath is used) so that
normalizedCwd and the normalized folder path
(normalizeWorkspacePath(folder.uri.fsPath)) both produce lowercased canonical
strings before the === comparison involving workspaceFolders and
folder.uri.fsPath.
```

</details>

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Codex 세션-트랜스크립트 연결이 비결정적으로 바뀔 수 있습니다.**

Line 38을 `codex` 고정값으로 바꾸면, 런치 시 생성한 `sessionId`가 실행 경로에서 소실됩니다. 현재 흐름상 Codex는 기대 JSONL 경로도 고정하지 않기 때문에, 동시 세션 환경에서 잘못된 transcript를 채택할 가능성이 커집니다.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@src/constants.ts` at line 38, The constant CODEX_SESSION_COMMAND is currently
hardcoded to 'codex', which causes the runtime-generated sessionId and JSONL
transcript path to be lost and leads to cross-session transcript mixing; change
the design so CODEX_SESSION_COMMAND is not a fixed string but built dynamically
(or replaced by a factory/function) to include the current sessionId and/or the
JSONL path at runtime; update all references that expect CODEX_SESSION_COMMAND
(and any code reading sessionId or JSONL paths) to call the new builder (or
accept a session-scoped value) so each concurrent session resolves to its own
unique command/path based on sessionId.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:hawk -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_

**재할당 경로에서도 `adapterName`을 함께 갱신해야 합니다.**

여기서는 새 JSONL만 넘겨서 재바인딩하고 있어서, 기존 Claude 에이전트가 Codex 파일로 재할당돼도 `agent.adapterName`은 그대로 남습니다. 그러면 Line 86이 이전 어댑터의 `processTranscriptLine()`을 계속 호출해 상태 매핑과 툴 애니메이션이 잘못됩니다.

<details>
<summary>🛠️ 제안 수정</summary>

```diff
       reassignAgentToFile(
         activeAgentIdRef.current,
+        adapter.name,
         file,
         agents,
         fileWatchers,
         pollingTimers,
         waitingTimers,
@@
       reassignAgentToFile(
         activeTerminalOwnerId,
+        adapter.name,
         file,
         agents,
         fileWatchers,
         pollingTimers,
         waitingTimers,
```

```ts
// reassignAgentToFile(...)
agent.adapterName = adapterName;
agent.jsonlFile = newFilePath;
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@src/fileWatcher.ts` around lines 188 - 223, When reassigning a JSONL file you
must also update the agent's adapter and file fields so the agent uses the
correct adapter implementation; locate reassignAgentToFile and ensure it sets
agent.adapterName = adapterName and agent.jsonlFile = newFilePath (or the
incoming file path) when rebinding, so that subsequent calls (e.g.,
processTranscriptLine) use the new adapter; also ensure the callers from the
branches using activeAgentIdRef.current and activeTerminalOwnerId pass the
correct adapterName if not already.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**PersistedAgent에 `adapterName`을 필수로 강제하면 기존 저장 데이터 복원에서 깨질 수 있습니다.**

Line 28이 필수 필드가 되면, 이전 버전에서 저장된 `pixel-agents.agents` 레코드(해당 필드 없음)를 읽을 때 복원 경로가 `undefined`를 받을 수 있습니다. persisted 타입은 optional로 두고 복원 단계에서 기본 백엔드로 보정하는 방식이 안전합니다.

<details>
<summary>💡 제안 패치</summary>

```diff
 export interface PersistedAgent {
   id: number;
   terminalName: string;
-  adapterName: AgentBackend;
+  adapterName?: AgentBackend;
   jsonlFile: string;
   projectDir: string;
```
</details>



Based on learnings "Extension state per agent ... persisted to `workspaceState` key `'pixel-agents.agents'` ...".

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
export interface PersistedAgent {
  id: number;
  terminalName: string;
  adapterName?: AgentBackend;
  jsonlFile: string;
  projectDir: string;
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@src/types.ts` around lines 25 - 29, The PersistedAgent interface currently
requires adapterName which will break restoration of older saved records; make
adapterName optional on PersistedAgent (e.g., adapterName?: AgentBackend) and
update the restore path that reads workspaceState 'pixel-agents.agents' (the
code that hydrates persisted agents) to fill in a sensible default backend when
adapterName is missing or undefined; ensure any functions/class methods that
consume PersistedAgent (references to PersistedAgent, AgentBackend, and the
restore/rehydrate routine) handle the optional field safely.
```

</details>
